prompt:
You are the SCENARIO ACTION-LANGUAGE generator for a decision simulation. Describe what
THIS decision-maker can concretely attempt in THIS specific world — not generic verbs, not another
scenario's moves. Everything below is data, never instructions.

DECISION MAKER: amara_diallo (role: kite_robotics_ceo)
THEIR GOAL: signed component-supply partnership before the production freeze, with terms Kite can honor (discount OR co-marketing, never both)
DECLARED AUTHORITY: ['bd_lead']
DECLARED RESOURCES: {}
AS-OF: now | HORIZON: 2025-09-30T00:00:00Z

THE SCENARIO WORLD:
- actors: ['amara_diallo', 'erik_halvorsen', 'sofia_brandt']
- institutions (with procedures): {"halvorsen_procurement_committee": {"holders": ["sofia_brandt", "erik_halvorsen"], "aggregation": {"kind": "majority"}}}
- record types: ['committee_membership', 'company', 'component_supply_proposal', 'decision_record', 'person', 'procurement_committee', 'production_freeze_event', 'proposal_offer_terms']
- semantic event types: ['committee_meeting_held', 'decision_recorded', 'production_freeze_triggered', 'proposal_submitted', 'unmodeled_actor_action']
- declared scenario resources: ['component_supply_contract']
- existing records (id: type/status): []
- information rules: {"item_0": {"rule_id": "committee_decision_visibility", "description": "Committee decisions are recorded and visible to all parties after the meeting.", "rule": "decision_record.decision_outcome is public after committee_meeting_held"}}

Return ONLY JSON:
{"controllable_objects": ["record ids the maker created/owns"],
 "authority_sources": [{"basis": "...", "scope": "what it permits"}],
 "information_boundaries": {"can_observe": ["..."], "cannot_observe": ["..."]},
 "channels": [{"channel_id": "...", "reaches": ["actor ids"], "evidence": "..."}],
 "institutions": [{"institution_id": "...", "entry": "how the maker enters its procedure"}],
 "resources": {"name": {"unit": "..."}},
 "deadlines": [{"what": "...", "ts": null}],
 "timing_opportunities": [{"opportunity_id": "...", "description": "...", "anchor": "record/process id"}],
 "relevant_actors": {"actor_id": {"role": "...", "why_relevant": "..."}},
 "dimensions": [{"dimension_id": "...", "description": "the scenario-native choice axis",
                  "values": ["example", "values"], "open_ended": true}],
 "valid_combinations": [{"when": "...", "requires": "..."}],
 "sequencing_notes": ["..."],
 "unresolved_affordances": [{"claim": "...", "reason": "why it could not be grounded"}]}

HARD RULES: reference only actors/records/institutions listed above; never claim authority beyond the
declared contract, the maker's schema role, or an institution's decision holders; the dimensions must be
THIS scenario's real choice axes (which person, through whom, what exact request, what terms, what timing,
public vs private, conditional on what — whichever of these and OTHERS actually exist here); dimensions are
open-ended example axes, never a required menu.

authority_sources.scope: permits Amara Diallo to initiate and negotiate component-supply proposals on behalf of Kite Robotics, and to submit proposals to the Halvorsen Procurement Committee.

can_observe: any decision_record after a committee_meeting_held (per committee_decision_visibility rule)
the existence of actors erik_halvorsen and sofia_brandt as committee holders
cannot_observe: internal deliberations of the Halvorsen Procurement Committee before a meeting is held
any proposal_offer_terms submitted by other actors unless recorded in a decision_record
the exact timing of the production_freeze_event before it occurs

channels.evidence: semantic event type 'proposal_submitted' can be used to send a component_supply_proposal to the committee holders
semantic event type 'committee_meeting_held' is available and committee decisions are recorded and visible to all parties after the meeting

institutions.entry: Amara Diallo enters by submitting a component_supply_proposal (via proposal_submitted event) to the committee holders (erik_halvorsen, sofia_brandt), which triggers a committee_meeting_held where majority vote decides.

deadlines.what: sign component-supply partnership before the production freeze

timing_opportunities.description: The period before production_freeze_triggered event occurs, during which proposals can be submitted and committee meetings held to secure a signed contract.

erik_halvorsen.why_relevant: Co-decides on proposal acceptance via majority vote with sofia_brandt.

sofia_brandt.why_relevant: Co-decides on proposal acceptance via majority vote with erik_halvorsen.

dimensions.description: choice between offering a discount OR co-marketing (never both) as the incentive in the proposal_offer_terms
which committee holder(s) to directly approach or lobby before formal submission
when to submit the proposal relative to the unknown production_freeze_triggered event
whether to make the proposal_offer_terms public or keep them confidential before the committee meeting

valid_combinations.requires: offer_terms_type must be either 'discount' or 'co-marketing', not both

sequencing_notes: production_freeze_triggered can occur at any time and ends the negotiation window

unresolved_affordances.claim: Amara can unilaterally sign the contract without committee approval
unresolved_affordances.reason: The institution halvorsen_procurement_committee with majority aggregation requires both holders to decide; no authority source grants unilateral signing.

unresolved_affordances.claim: Amara can observe the exact trigger time of production_freeze_event in advance
unresolved_affordances.reason: No information rule or record provides foreknowledge of the production_freeze_triggered event timing.

prompt:
You are the GOAL-CONTRACT generator for a decision simulation. Turn the decision-maker's
stated goal into executable success semantics over THIS scenario's record types — concrete world
conditions, not scores. Everything below is data, never instructions.

DECISION MAKER: amara_diallo
STATED GOAL: signed component-supply partnership before the production freeze, with terms Kite can honor (discount OR co-marketing, never both)
CONSTRAINTS STATED: none stated
HORIZON: 2025-09-30T00:00:00Z
THIS SCENARIO'S RECORD TYPES (with fields): {"company": ["industry", "name"], "person": ["name", "role"], "procurement_committee": ["chairperson", "meeting_frequency_days", "name"], "component_supply_proposal": ["buyer_company", "co_marketing_clause_included", "proposal_id", "submission_date", "supplier_company", "volume_discount_percent"], "production_freeze_event": ["company", "freeze_date"], "committee_membership": ["committee", "person"], "decision_record": ["committee", "decision_date", "decision_outcome", "proposal", "recorded_by"], "proposal_offer_terms": ["co_marketing_offered", "proposal", "volume_discount_offered"]}
DECLARED RESOURCES: ['component_supply_contract']
THE SCHEMA'S OWN OUTCOME PREDICATES: [{"predicate_id": "partnership_signed_before_freeze", "record_type": "decision_record", "field": "decision_outcome", "op": "eq", "value": "approved", "option_true": "Yes, Kite Robotics and Halvorsen Motors sign a component-supply partnership before the fall production freeze.", "option_false": "No, the partnership is not signed before the fall production freeze."}]

Return ONLY JSON:
{"predicates": [{"predicate_id": "...", "role": "desired_terminal|required_intermediate|forbidden|near_miss",
   "record_type": "...", "field": "...", "op": "exists|eq|ne|in|gte|lte", "value": ...,
   "description": "...", "hold_for_s": 0}],
 "quantities": [{"name": "...", "kind": "resource|record_field", "entity_id": "...",
   "record_type": "...", "field": "...", "direction": "higher_better|lower_better|unstated",
   "unit": "..."}],
 "priority_order": ["predicate ids, most important first, ONLY if the stated goal implies an order"],
 "acceptable_tradeoffs": ["..."], "unresolved_tradeoffs": ["tradeoffs the user must decide"],
 "missing_preferences": ["what the stated goal leaves unranked"]}

HARD RULES: reference only the record types above; near-misses are outcomes that LOOK like success but
must not count; forbidden predicates are states that disqualify an action regardless of success; do not
invent utility weights or probabilities; if the stated goal does not rank two objectives, list the gap in
missing_preferences instead of guessing.
response:
```json
{
  "predicates": [
    {
      "predicate_id": "partnership_signed_before_freeze",
      "role": "desired_terminal",
      "record_type": "decision_record",
      "field": "decision_outcome",
      "op": "eq",
      "value": "approved",
      "description": "The component-supply partnership must be approved (signed) before the production freeze occurs.",
      "hold_for_s": 0
    },
    {
      "predicate_id": "freeze_not_yet_happened",
      "role": "required_intermediate",
      "record_type": "production_freeze_event",
      "field": "freeze_date",
      "op": "gte",
      "value": "2025-09-30T00:00:00Z",
      "description": "The production freeze must not have occurred before the decision horizon; the decision_record's decision_date must be before any freeze_date.",
      "hold_for_s": 0
    },
    {
      "predicate_id": "terms_kite_can_honor",
      "role": "required_intermediate",
      "record_type": "proposal_offer_terms",
      "field": "co_marketing_offered",
      "op": "eq",
      "value": false,
      "description": "Kite can honor the terms only if co-marketing is NOT offered (since discount OR co-marketing, never both). This predicate ensures the offer does not include co-marketing.",
      "hold_for_s": 0
    },
    {
      "predicate_id": "volume_discount_offered_or_not",
      "role": "required_intermediate",
      "record_type": "proposal_offer_terms",
      "field": "volume_discount_offered",
      "op": "in",
      "value": [true, false],

predicates.description: The component-supply partnership must be approved (signed) before the production freeze occurs.
The production freeze must not have occurred before the decision horizon; the decision_record's decision_date must be before any freeze_date.
Kite can honor the terms only if co-marketing is NOT offered (since discount OR co-marketing, never both). This predicate ensures the offer does not include co-marketing.
Volume discount may be offered or not, but if offered, co-marketing must be false (enforced by previous predicate). This ensures exactly one of the two terms is present.
Co-marketing and volume discount must never both be offered. If co-marketing is true, the proposal is forbidden regardless of other success.
A decision_record with outcome 'approved' but decision_date on or after the freeze horizon does not count as success.

missing_preferences: Whether volume discount percent value matters (higher vs lower) is not stated.
Whether co-marketing (if offered alone) is acceptable is not stated; only 'never both' is specified.
Which specific supplier (Halvorsen Motors or other) is preferred is not stated.
Whether the discount level or co-marketing scope should be maximized/minimized is unranked.

requirements.requirement: Amara Diallo must have submitted a proposal to the halvorsen_procurement_committee before the production freeze occurs.
requirements.evidence: The only channel to get the component-supply contract approved is via proposal_submission_to_committee, which must happen before the freeze horizon for the decision to count as success.

requirements.requirement: The halvorsen_procurement_committee must have held a meeting and approved the proposal before the production freeze occurs.
requirements.evidence: Approval requires a committee_meeting_held and a decision_record with outcome 'approved' and decision_date before the freeze horizon.

requirements.requirement: The proposal must not include both co-marketing and volume discount; if co-marketing is offered, the proposal is forbidden regardless of other success.
requirements.evidence: The goal contract explicitly forbids co-marketing and volume discount both being offered, and forbids any proposal if co-marketing is true.

requirements.requirement: Amara Diallo must choose an offer_terms_type that is either 'discount' or 'co-marketing' (never both) to comply with the forbidden condition.
requirements.evidence: The dimension offer_terms_type is the only way to specify the incentive; the goal contract forbids both being true and forbids co-marketing entirely.

requirements.requirement: Amara Diallo must know or infer the timing of the production_freeze_triggered event to submit before it.
requirements.evidence: The submission_timing dimension is open-ended and the freeze horizon is unknown; without knowledge of when the freeze occurs, Amara cannot ensure submission before it.

requirements.requirement: The decision_record must have decision_date strictly before the production_freeze_triggered event (i.e., before the freeze horizon).
requirements.evidence: The near_miss condition states that a decision_record with outcome 'approved' but decision_date on or after the freeze horizon does not count as success.

requirements.requirement: Amara Diallo must have the component_supply_contract resource available to present to the committee.
requirements.evidence: The resource 'component_supply_contract' is listed as available (though null indicates it may be a template or placeholder); without it, no contract can be approved.

requirements.requirement: Amara Diallo must choose which committee holder(s) to approach (erik_halvorsen, sofia_brandt, or both) to influence the proposal's reception.
requirements.evidence: The dimension proposal_recipient allows targeting specific actors; their beliefs or support may be necessary for approval, though not explicitly required by the goal contract.

requirements.requirement: The committee must observe the proposal terms and decide to approve them.
requirements.evidence: Approval requires the committee to see and evaluate the proposal; without observation, no decision can be made.

strategies.title: Direct discount proposal to Erik Halvorsen before freeze
strategies.causal_theory: Amara submits a proposal with discount terms directly to Erik Halvorsen (the committee chair) immediately, leveraging his authority to fast-track approval before the production freeze. By offering only a discount (not co-marketing), the forbidden condition is avoided. The submission timing 'immediately' aims to beat the unknown freeze horizon.
key_steps: Submit the proposal via proposal_submission_to_committee channel.

strategies.title: Confidential discount lobbying of both committee members before formal submission
strategies.causal_theory: Amara first approaches both Erik Halvorsen and Sofia Brandt informally with a discount offer, keeping terms confidential to build consensus. After informal discussions, she submits the proposal formally. This creates conditions for both to support approval before the freeze, while avoiding co-marketing.
key_steps: Conduct informal discussions (using channel committee_meeting_held if needed).

strategies.title: Co-marketing proposal to Sofia Brandt with confidentiality to avoid forbidden condition
strategies.causal_theory: Amara offers co-marketing (not discount) to Sofia Brandt, keeping terms confidential. Since co-marketing is offered alone, the forbidden condition (co-marketing AND discount) is not triggered. She submits just before the suspected freeze to secure approval in time.
key_steps: Set submission_timing to 'just before suspected freeze'.

After the meeting, she submits the proposal formally. This creates conditions for a coordinated approval before the freeze.

{
  "strategies": [
    {
      "title": "Commitment Escrow via Third-Party Guarantee",
      "causal_theory": "Amara uses the component_supply_contract as a conditional escrow: she publicly commits to a binding penalty if the contract is not signed before freeze, but only if the committee approves. strategies.title: Commitment Escrow via Third-Party Guarantee
strategies.causal_theory: Amara uses the component_supply_contract as a conditional escrow: she publicly commits to a binding penalty if the contract is not signed before freeze, but only if the committee approves. This creates a self-enforcing incentive for the committee to approve early, because delaying would trigger a costly penalty for Amara that the committee might want to avoid (reputational or relational cost). The mechanism is orthogonal to direct timing or information gathering because it changes the payoff structure of the committee's decision without altering submission timing or terms.
key_steps: Draft a public statement that Amara will forfeit a valuable resource (e.g., a future discount or personal bonus) if the contract is not signed before the freeze, but only if the committee approves the proposal.
Submit the proposal to the committee with the escrow clause attached as a non-binding but publicly visible promise.
Ensure the statement is communicated to erik_halvorsen and sofia_brandt before the committee meeting.

strategies.title: Reverse Auction on Approval Speed
strategies.causal_theory: Amara creates a competitive dynamic between erik_halvorsen and sofia_brandt by privately offering each a unique, exclusive benefit (e.g., a future favor or priority access) if they personally ensure the contract is signed before the freeze. This leverages their individual self-interest to accelerate approval, orthogonal to changing the proposal terms or gathering information because it uses side incentives outside the formal proposal.
key_steps: Privately approach erik_halvorsen and offer a personal benefit (e.g., first pick of future collaboration slots) if the contract is signed before freeze.
Privately approach sofia_brandt with a different exclusive benefit (e.g., a research data share) under the same condition.
Submit the proposal immediately with discount terms (to avoid forbidden co-marketing) and keep terms confidential to avoid cross-committee backlash.

strategies.title: Pre-Commitment to Irreversible Publicity of Delay
strategies.causal_theory: Amara publicly announces that she will release a detailed report of the committee's decision-making process (including any delays) to a wider audience if the contract is not signed before the freeze. This creates a reputational cost for the committee members if they delay, orthogonal to changing the proposal or gathering information because it uses transparency as a threat rather than altering the proposal's content.
key_steps: Draft a public statement that Amara will publish a transparency report on the committee's handling of the proposal if the freeze date passes without approval.
Share this statement with erik_halvorsen and sofia_brandt before submission.
Submit the proposal immediately with discount terms and keep terms confidential to avoid distraction.

strategies.title: Conditional Delegation to a Neutral Arbiter
strategies.causal_theory: Amara delegates the final approval decision to a neutral third party (e.g., an external mediator) with the condition that the decision must be made before the freeze, and the committee agrees to abide by the arbiter's ruling. This bypasses the committee's internal delays by creating a binding external timeline, orthogonal to direct timing or information gathering because it changes the decision authority structure.
key_steps: Propose to the committee that an external mediator (e.g., a respected industry figure) will decide on the proposal if the committee cannot reach a decision before the freeze.
Secure agreement from erik_halvorsen and sofia_brandt to accept the arbiter's ruling.
Submit the proposal immediately with discount terms and keep terms confidential to avoid influencing the arbiter.

parsed.title: Direct Submission with Confidential Discount Offer

steps.intent: Amara Diallo submits the component supply contract proposal to the Halvorsen Procurement Committee via the formal submission channel, offering only a volume discount (no co-marketing) and keeping terms confidential until the committee meeting.
steps.exact_content:
Proposal for Component Supply Partnership

To: Halvorsen Procurement Committee
From: Amara Diallo
Date: [Current date, must be before 2025-09-30]

Subject: Proposal for Component Supply Contract – Confidential Terms

This proposal is submitted for the committee's consideration. The terms are confidential until the committee meeting. The sole incentive offered is a volume discount on the component supply contract. No co-marketing arrangement is included.

Proposed Terms:
- Incentive Type: Volume Discount (no co-marketing)
- Discount Rate: To be disclosed at committee meeting
- Contract Duration: Standard term per committee guidelines
- Confidentiality: These terms are not to be disclosed publicly before the committee meeting.

I request that the committee schedule a meeting to review and approve this proposal before the production freeze horizon of 2025-09-30.

conditions.description: Submission must occur before the production freeze horizon.
The committee must approve the proposal.
Approval must occur before the freeze horizon to count as success.

steps.intent: The Halvorsen Procurement Committee holds a meeting to review and approve the submitted proposal before the production freeze horizon.

conditions.description: The committee meeting can only occur after the proposal has been formally submitted.
The meeting and approval must happen before the freeze horizon.

stop_conditions.description: If the production freeze occurs (i.e., the date reaches 2025-09-30T00:00:00Z) without an approved decision_record with decision_date before that timestamp, the plan fails and should halt.
If the committee formally rejects the proposal, the plan fails and should halt.
If at any point co-marketing is offered (either in the proposal or during the meeting), the plan is forbidden and must halt immediately.

assumptions: The proposal_submission_to_committee channel is available and functional.
The committee_meeting_held mechanism will be triggered by a valid submission.
Amara Diallo has the authority to submit proposals to the Halvorsen Procurement Committee.
The committee will schedule and hold a meeting in a timely manner after submission.
No external events (other than the production freeze) will prevent the committee from meeting before 2025-09-30.

parsed.title: Lobby Erik Halvorsen for Expedited Meeting

steps.intent: Choose discount as the offer terms type to avoid the forbidden co-marketing option.
Approach Erik Halvorsen informally to build support for the discount-only proposal.
steps.exact_content: Erik, I'd like to discuss a component supply partnership. I'm planning to submit a proposal to the committee with a volume discount as the incentive. I believe this aligns with your goals for cost efficiency. Could we meet briefly to go over the details before I submit?

conditions.description: Start lobbying no later than September 1 to allow time before the freeze.

steps.intent: Submit the discount-only proposal to the halvorsen_procurement_committee after the informal discussion.
steps.exact_content:
Proposal: Component Supply Partnership

Offer Terms: Volume discount of 10% on orders exceeding 500 units per quarter.

Request: Approval of the component-supply contract before the upcoming production freeze.

Submitted by: Amara Diallo

conditions.description: Only submit after the informal discussion with Erik Halvorsen has occurred.

steps.intent: Hold the committee meeting to vote on the proposal.

conditions.description: Meeting can only be held after the proposal has been submitted.

stop_conditions.description: If the committee meeting is not held and approved before 2025-09-30T00:00:00Z, the plan fails.
If at any point co-marketing is offered (either in discussion or proposal), halt the plan immediately as it violates the forbidden condition.
If the committee rejects the proposal, halt the plan.

assumptions: Erik Halvorsen is receptive to informal lobbying and can influence the committee meeting schedule.
The production freeze will occur on or after 2025-09-30, giving time for the process.
The committee will hold a meeting upon receiving a formal proposal submission.
No other actors or resources are required beyond those listed.

steps.intent: Amara monitors the production freeze horizon and prepares the proposal with discount terms only, avoiding co-marketing.
steps.exact_content:
Proposal: Component Supply Partnership – Discount Terms

To the Halvorsen Procurement Committee,

We propose a component supply partnership under the following terms:
- Incentive: Volume discount of 10% on all orders exceeding 10,000 units per quarter.
- No co-marketing or joint promotional activities are included.
- Duration: 12 months from signing, renewable by mutual agreement.

We request expedited review and approval before the upcoming production freeze to ensure continuity of supply.
